source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Mountaineers often use a rope to lower themselves down the face of a cliff (this is called \textit{rappelling}). They do this with their body nearly horizontal and their feet pushing against the cliff. Suppose that an \( 82.0\, \text{kg} \) climber, who is \( 1.90\, \text{m} \) tall and has a center of gravity \( 1.1\, \text{m} \) from his feet, rappels down a vertical cliff with his body raised \( 35.0^\circ \) above the horizontal. He holds the rope \( 1.40\, \text{m} \) from his feet, and it makes a \( 25.0^\circ \) angle with the cliff face. What minimum coefficient of static friction is needed to prevent the climber’s feet from slipping on the cliff face if he has one foot at a time against the cliff?

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is registered as image-reliant
The question involves spatial geometry and force directions (body angle, rope angle, vertical cliff), which are commonly supported by a diagram
An image asset exists and registry_passed=true, so visual context is available
However, the text explicitly states the key angles and distances, so dependency is not scored at the maximum
multi_step: The problem is a mechanics/statics setup requiring combination of multiple quantities: mass, height, center of gravity location, rope contact point, and two angles
Solving for minimum static friction typically requires more than one relation, such as force balance plus torque balance
The target quantity is not directly stated and must be derived from intermediate physical quantities (e.g. normal force / friction relation)
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single explicit value: 1.48
Multiple-choice options are provided, allowing direct answer matching
The problem statement gives concrete numerical inputs, making a deterministic check feasible

risk_flags: text_contains_many geometric details, so actual image necessity may be lower than requires_image suggests

decision_reasons: 资产完整：题干、答案和图像均存在，且 registry_passed=true、issues 为空
综合质量较高：multi_step_score=0.84，verifiability_score=0.96，适合进入候选池
题目核心语义可恢复性强：文本提供了明确的几何与数值条件，图像也可作为补充支撑
虽有“图像必要性可能被高估”的风险提示，但这不构成核心语义缺失或样本不可用
